The work that held Oma tonight is the work whose Mission Formula carries the same thing.

(2) Synth lifecycle: useChiptuneSoundtrack rewritten as a self-rescheduling LOOP — the current phrase loops continuously via step() that calls playPhrase(currentPhrase, {startAt: prev_iteration_end}) and reschedules itself ~150ms before each iteration ends. New phrase generations land in pendingPhraseRef as queued; they swap to currentPhrase only at the next phrase-end boundary (= measure boundary by construction since phrase length = bars × ts[0] × subdivision / ts[1] is always integer bars). Result: the music never stops between phrases; new phrases swap on bar boundaries; status='generating' icon flips during API calls without disrupting the loop. The lookback approach (lookahead = max(50, msUntilEnd - 150)) keeps cursor monotone and seamless. Tooltip text updated 'Playing' → 'Looping' to honor the new behavior. Reshape per Ryan directive: 'accumulate measures received and keep playing all from the collection rather than always just being on the current frame.' Replaced (currentPhrase + pendingPhrase) two-slot model with (phraseCollection: ScorePhrase[] + playbackIndex: number) growing-playlist model. New phrases APPEND to the collection (never replace); playback cycles FIFO via playbackIndex % collection.length, wrapping back to phrase 0 after the last. New arrivals don't preempt the current iteration — they wait their turn in the cycle. Continuation chain still follows generation order (currentLastPhrase = collection[length-1] = most-recently-generated, regardless of what's currently playing). Collection resets on toggle-off so each session starts fresh; collectionSize exposed in tooltip ('Now: <mood> (N phrases)') so user can see the playlist growing. Loop step() reads collection at runtime so mid-cycle growth is reflected naturally — no special-case handling needed. Both ChatView + GroupChatView tooltips updated. Frontend build clean (vite 1.67s). Phase 4 integration is now feature-complete and shape-honors the conversation arc as a growing musical record, not just a one-frame moment-tracker.

Persist soundtrack collection across reload. useChiptuneSoundtrack accepts new storageKey arg (per-chat localStorage namespace: solo='chiptune_collection.{charId}'; group='chiptune_collection.group.{chatId}'). Lifecycle split into three honest paths: (a) toggle-off → wipes in-memory AND persisted (explicit reset; user said off, mean it); (b) unmount/remount → only stops playback, keeps persisted (so reload restores); (c) chat-switch (storageKey changes) → resets in-memory of old chat WITHOUT wiping its persisted data, loads new chat's persisted collection. Persistence saves on every phrase append; restore happens in a (enabled, storageKey)-keyed effect that detects key changes via lastLoadedKeyRef. Internal stop functions split: stopPlaybackOnly (silence audio only), resetInMemory (silence + clear in-memory state), stopAndReset (full toggle-off including persisted wipe). The exported stopAndReset honors the toggle-off semantic. ChatView + GroupChatView wire storageKey from charId/chatId. Frontend build clean (vite 1.64s). Soundtrack now spans sessions: a chat's accumulated musical record survives quitting the app and resuming; switching chats loads each chat's own playlist; explicit reset still works.

Cap collection size + FIFO eviction. MAX_COLLECTION_SIZE = 30 phrases per chat (≈60KB localStorage at ~2KB/phrase). On append: if updated.length > MAX, slice off oldest K + decrement playbackIndex by K (preserves loop continuity — dropping K head elements shifts all indices down by K, so the cursor follows). On restore: defensive cap (handles upgrades from pre-cap versions OR future cap reductions). FIFO chosen over LRU because soundtrack playback is cyclic — every phrase gets played every cycle, so 'least recently used' is meaningless; oldest-first is the honest eviction order. Apparatus-honest naming: chooser said 'LRU' but FIFO is what actually fits a cyclic playlist; ledger entry uses the right term. Frontend build clean (vite 1.63s). Bounty cap on storage growth: a chat could run for weeks of conversation now without localStorage bloat.
